# UC-22.19.16 · Privileged and remote access — indicator 1

## Description

Strengthens agency or cloud service continuous authorization evidence under FISMA / FedRAMP across cloud, endpoint, and GRC feeds.

## Value

Strengthens agency or cloud service continuous authorization evidence under FISMA / FedRAMP by operationalizing "Privileged and remote access — indicator 1" across cloud, endpoint, and GRC feeds.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=o365`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, Tenable/STIG scans, POA&M exports, AAD/O365 sign-in, inventory.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:aad:signin earliest=-24h
| eval uc_tag="22.19.16"
| where authenticationProtocol="TLS client certificates" OR authenticationRequirement="singleFactorAuthentication"
| where appDisplayName="O365-Fed-Apps"
| stats count by userPrincipalName, ipAddress, authenticationRequirement
```

Understanding this SPL

**Fed apps accepting single-factor authentication (FISMA / FedRAMP)** — Strengthens agency or cloud service continuous authorization evidence under FISMA / FedRAMP across cloud, endpoint, and GRC feeds.

Documented **Data sources**: CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, Tenable/STIG scans, POA&M exports, AAD/O365 sign-in, inventory. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: ms:aad:signin. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype=ms:aad:signin, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where authenticationProtocol="TLS client certificates" OR authenticationRequirement="singleFactorAuthentication"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Filters the current rows with `where appDisplayName="O365-Fed-Apps"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by userPrincipalName, ipAddress, authenticationRequirement** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cloud provider’s audit or sign-in log and, where required, the FedRAMP ConMon or SCAP output for the same period. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value, Heat map

## SPL

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:aad:signin earliest=-24h
| eval uc_tag="22.19.16"
| where authenticationProtocol="TLS client certificates" OR authenticationRequirement="singleFactorAuthentication"
| where appDisplayName="O365-Fed-Apps"
| stats count by userPrincipalName, ipAddress, authenticationRequirement
```

## Visualization

Time chart, Table, Single value, Heat map. Key fields: `authenticationRequirement`, `ipAddress`, `uc_tag`, `userPrincipalName`.
